Title: Divergent MYB paralogs determine spatial distribution of linalool mediated by JA and DNA demethylation participating in aroma formation and cold tolerance of tea plants

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes floral aroma/tea quality and enhances cold tolerance by reducing ROS and participating in downstream cold-response pathways
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and young leaves; increases during tea withering/processing
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Determining component of linalool concentration in flowers and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated during withering, by MeJA, by cold treatment, and by 5-azaC; reduced by DIECA and by knockdown of upstream regulators
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tea withering/processing; MeJA treatment; JA inhibitor DIECA; cold stress; 5-azacytidine treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; promoter Y1H; EMSA; transient promoter activation in Arabidopsis protoplasts; GUS reporter in tobacco; upstream gene overexpression and asODN knockdown
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes linalool accumulation; elevated CsTPS76 expression is associated with increased linalool during processing and cold response
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsTPS76 is the direct linalool synthase and determining component of linalool concentration in tea flowers and leaves. 2. Its promoter is directly bound and activated by CsMYB68, CsMYB147, CsMYB148, CsMYB193, and CsMYC2, with stronger activation by MYC2-MYB complexes. 3. Its expression rises during withering, MeJA treatment, cold stress, and 5-azaC treatment, paralleling linalool accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Direct engineering target to enhance floral aroma and potentially improve cold tolerance; likely most effective when stacked with positive regulators such as CsMYB68/CsMYB147/CsMYC2 or combined with JA-responsive regulatory modules
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression analyses in natural population and stress conditions; methylome information from previous cold-stress study referenced in paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although CsTPS76 is central in both flowers and leaves, tissue specificity of linalool accumulation is largely explained by divergent upstream MYB regulators rather than reported methylation changes of CsTPS76 itself under cold stress.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides floral aroma and contributes to cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding and cooperative activation with CsMYC2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator in tea leaves and also active in flowers; stress-responsive controller of leaf linalool biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts downstream of JA signalling and DNA demethylation under cold stress; no direct feedback loop established
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonic acid/MeJA; cold stress; DNA demethylation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYC2-MYB activation complex with CsMYC2; complex formation is interfered by CsJAZ12
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes CsTPS76 expression and linalool accumulation; positively contributes to cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYB68 is highly expressed in flowers and apical buds and is present in leaves, unlike flower-specific paralogs. 2. Overexpression induced linalool accumulation in tobacco and upregulated CsTPS76 in tea hairy roots; joint knockdown with CsMYB147 reduced CsTPS76 and linalool in tea leaves. 3. CsMYB68 directly binds the CsTPS76 promoter, acts synergistically with CsMYC2, is induced by JA/withering/cold, and is upregulated by DNA demethylation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in tea flowers and apical buds; expressed in leaves; induced under biotic stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increases during withering, MeJA treatment, cold stress, and 5-azaC treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in tobacco; tea hairy root overexpression; asODN knockdown; Y1H; EMSA; Y2H; BiFC; GST pull-down; transient transactivation assays; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Withering during white tea processing; MeJA treatment; DIECA treatment; cold stress; 5-azacytidine treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in tobacco overexpression; native CsTPS76 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Cold stress decreased methylation of CsMYB68 locus; 5-azaC increased CsMYB68 expression, supporting repression by DNA methylation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate to improve tea aroma in leaves and enhance cold tolerance, especially in combination with CsMYC2 or by targeted demethylation/JA-responsive activation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq across natural population and stress treatments; methylation analysis under cold stress from prior dataset
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the younger MYB paralogs; appears especially important for leaf-specific/stress-induced linalool regulation compared with flower-specific CsMYB148/CsMYB193.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides floral aroma and contributes to cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding and cooperative activation with CsMYC2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator in tea leaves and flowers, especially stress-responsive in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts downstream of JA signalling and DNA demethylation under cold stress; no direct feedback loop established
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonic acid/MeJA; cold stress; DNA demethylation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYC2-MYB activation complex with CsMYC2; complex formation is interfered by CsJAZ12
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes CsTPS76 expression and linalool accumulation; positively contributes to cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYB147 is highly expressed in flowers, fruits, roots and young leaves, supporting a broad spatial role. 2. Overexpression induced linalool accumulation in tobacco, and combined knockdown with CsMYB68 reduced CsTPS76 expression and linalool in tea leaves. 3. CsMYB147 directly binds the CsTPS76 promoter, acts synergistically with CsMYC2, is induced by JA/withering/cold, and is upregulated by demethylation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers, fruits, roots and young leaves; induced under biotic stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increases during withering, MeJA treatment, cold stress, and 5-azaC treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in tobacco; asODN knockdown with CsMYB68; Y1H; EMSA; Y2H; BiFC; GST pull-down; transient transactivation assays; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Withering during white tea processing; MeJA treatment; DIECA treatment; cold stress; 5-azacytidine treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in tobacco overexpression; native CsTPS76 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Cold stress decreased methylation of CsMYB147 locus; 5-azaC increased CsMYB147 expression, supporting repression by DNA methylation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving aroma formation during processing and increasing cold resilience; likely best deployed with CsMYB68 and CsMYC2
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq across natural population and stress treatments; methylation analysis under cold stress from prior dataset
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with CsMYB68 constitutes the dominant leaf linalool regulator module in tea.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides floral aroma and contributes to cold tolerance-associated linalool pathway in flowers
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding and cooperative activation with CsMYC2
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts downstream of JA signalling in flowers; no direct feedback loop established
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonic acid/MeJA; cold stress in flowers (discussion); developmental flower specificity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYC2-MYB activation complex with CsMYC2; interaction also antagonized by CsJAZ12
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes CsTPS76 expression and linalool biosynthesis, especially in tea flowers
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYB148 is specifically expressed in tea flowers and absent from leaves. 2. Ectopic overexpression increased linalool in tobacco and upregulated CsTPS76 in tea hairy roots. 3. CsMYB148 directly binds the CsTPS76 promoter and cooperates with CsMYC2, while CsJAZ12 represses this activation by interfering with complex formation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not induced during leaf withering; discussed as MeJA/cold responsive in flowers
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in tobacco; tea hairy root overexpression; Y1H; EMSA; BiFC; GST pull-down; transient transactivation assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flower tissue context; MeJA treatment; cold stress (flower discussion)
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in tobacco overexpression; native CsTPS76 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No significant cold-induced methylation decrease reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for enhancing floral aroma traits, especially in flower-rich tissues or specialty tea aroma contexts; less suitable for leaf-focused engineering due to low leaf expression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Limited natural expression in leaves may constrain its utility for leaf aroma improvement unless ectopically expressed
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq tissue expression and natural population data
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Y2H did not detect interaction with CsMYC2, but BiFC and pull-down supported interaction.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding and cooperative activation with CsMYC2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Flower-specific positive regulator functioning redundantly with CsMYB148
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts downstream of JA signalling in flowers; no direct feedback loop established
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental flower specificity; JA-related regulation inferred from complex context
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYC2-MYB activation complex with CsMYC2; interaction also antagonized by CsJAZ12
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes linalool biosynthesis, especially in tea flowers
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYB193 is specifically expressed in tea flowers. 2. Ectopic overexpression increased linalool in tobacco. 3. CsMYB193 directly binds the CsTPS76 promoter, interacts with CsMYC2 and CsJAZ12, and contributes to activation of the linalool synthase promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not induced during leaf withering; less affected by MeJA/cold in flowers according to discussion
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in tobacco; Y1H; EMSA; Y2H; BiFC; GST pull-down; transient transactivation assays
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in tobacco overexpression; native CsTPS76 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No significant cold-induced methylation decrease reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for floral aroma enhancement, though likely secondary to CsMYB148 because of weaker stress responsiveness
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq tissue expression and natural population data
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Localized in both nucleus and cytoplasm, unlike the predominantly nuclear other MYBs.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides floral aroma and contributes to cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation and co-activation with MYB partners
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CsTPS76 and enhances activation by CsMYB68/CsMYB147/CsMYB148/CsMYB193
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central JA signal regulator linking JA signalling to linalool biosynthesis in leaves and flowers
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Operates downstream of JA signalling; no explicit feedback on JA established here
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: JA/JA-Ile accumulation; MeJA; cold stress; withering/processing
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYC2-MYB activation complexes with CsMYB68, CsMYB147, CsMYB148 and CsMYB193; inhibited by CsJAZ12
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes CsTPS76 expression, linalool accumulation, and cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYC2 directly binds the CsTPS76 promoter and activates its transcription. 2. Knockdown of CsMYC2 reduced CsTPS76 expression and linalool, whereas transient overexpression increased both in tea leaves. 3. CsMYC2 forms synergistic activation complexes with divergent MYB paralogs and connects JA signalling to processing-induced and cold-induced linalool biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in flowers and leaves; suppressed by DIECA; induced under cold and in JA-high contexts
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated by cold stress and associated with withering/JA activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: asODN knockdown; transient overexpression in tea leaves; Arabidopsis overexpression; Y1H; EMSA; Y2H; BiFC; GST pull-down; transient transactivation assays; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Withering; MeJA treatment; DIECA treatment; cold stress; JA-elevated transgenic context via CsLOX2
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in Arabidopsis/tea transient overexpression; native CsTPS76 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No significant cold-induced methylation decrease reported for CsMYC2
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering hub to coordinate aroma enhancement and stress tolerance, but deployment should consider broader JA-associated pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropic effects expected from JA signalling manipulation, though not directly quantified in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/heatmaps of JA pathway activity; referenced population transcriptome analyses
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Overexpression in Arabidopsis induced several endogenous monoterpene synthases (AtTPS11, AtTPS14, AtTPS21), supporting conserved function.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral aroma and cold tolerance-associated metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction-mediated repression by interfering MYC2-MYB complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses activation of CsTPS76 by MYC2-MYB complexes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CsMYC2-MYB complex; CsTPS76 promoter activation output
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major JAZ repressor of linalool biosynthesis in tea leaves and likely flowers
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Component of JA signalling repression module; stability inferred to decrease under JA/cold, releasing downstream activation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: JA signalling; withering-induced and cold-induced JA accumulation leading to JAZ degradation (model/discussion)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with CsMYC2 and all four MYBs; interferes formation/function of MYC2-MYB activation complexes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses CsTPS76 expression and linalool accumulation; negatively affects cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among leaf/flower-expressed JAZs, CsJAZ12 uniquely interacted with all four linalool-related MYBs and also with CsMYC2. 2. Overexpression suppressed monoterpene synthase genes in Arabidopsis, whereas knockdown in tea leaves increased CsTPS76 expression and linalool content. 3. CsJAZ12 represses linalool biosynthesis by blocking MYC2-MYB-mediated activation of the CsTPS76 promoter and negatively regulates cold tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in young leaves and developing flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Suppressed functionally by JA-activated conditions during processing/cold according to model
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H screening; BiFC; GST pull-down; Arabidopsis overexpression; asODN knockdown; luciferase transactivation assay; GUS reporter assay; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Withering/processing; JA signalling context; cold stress
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in Arabidopsis overexpression; native CsTPS76 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising repression target for CRISPR knockout or weakening to increase aroma and cold tolerance, but caution warranted because broad JA deregulation may alter defense/growth balance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible pleiotropic JA signalling effects if repression is broadly removed; not directly quantified in study
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the principal JAZ member among 13 family members for linalool regulation in this study.
